rationale: The citing paper uses Choi and Bell (2011) for more than a passing mention: it introduces that study in the literature review as evidence that "preference minorities" have distinct buying patterns (more online purchase, lower price sensitivity). Later, in its "Preference Minorities" section, it explicitly returns to Choi and Bell to motivate and interpret its own mechanism that harbingers have non-representative tastes and are more likely to buy niche items. This citation therefore helps frame the conceptual explanation for the paper's findings, not just provide generic background.
relation_description: The citing paper applies the preference-minority logic from Choi and Bell (2011) to a different problem, using it to explain why early buyers with atypical tastes can signal new product failure. It transfers that idea from online-channel adoption geography to customer-level new-product success prediction.
